source_jobs.title: Talent
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: We are hiring in Talent to own recruiting end-to-end as we scale. You will work directly with our CEO to hire across engineering, GTM, and operations, with a mandate to further build the team that runs talent at Strala.

Background: Strala is rebuilding the foundation of one of the world’s largest industries - insurance.

In less than a year, we reached multi seven-figure ARR and are growing 50% MOM. We raised $55M across two rounds from Tier 1 funds including Peter Thiel's Founders Fund, led by the partner who was early in OpenAI, Cognition and Scale AI. Our team comes from institutions like Harvard, Berkeley & Oxford and firms like Optiver, AMD & Palantir (+ICPC, NeurIPS backgrounds).

Role requirements: Located in or willing to relocate to San Francisco. In-person, five days a week.

You'll Thrive If:

 - You operate like a founder - you own outcomes, move fast, and figure it out

 - You are closer. Whether through recruiting, sales, or high-stakes persuasion, you've gotten talented people to say yes against real competition.

 - You have a high bar for talent and strong opinions on what great looks like - you can spot a .01% candidate in a 15-minute call

 - You hunt. You go far beyond inbound through targeted outreach, creative sourcing, and deep network leverage

 - You think in systems. You build infrastructure (ATS workflows, sourcing pipelines, scorecards, automations) that compounds over time

 - You write well and communicate clearly. Candidates remember talking to you

 - Your background is non-obvious. Consulting, banking, operating — if you’ve spent time somewhere that forged elite judgment and hustle, we don’t care that you've never touched an ATS.

What the role entails:

 - Own recruiting end-to-end across the entire company: engineering, GTM, claims, and operations. You report directly to our CEO

 - Personally source candidates for our most critical roles

 - Build out the recruiting function: hire and manage recruiters/sourcers, run agency relationships, define our interview processes

 - Operate our recruiting stack (Ashby + sourcing tools + automations) and continuously improve it

 - Own employer brand and our presence in the markets we recruit from

Our values:

 - Ambition: We want to win, and we need to be the best

 - Performance: Speed, system thinking, efficiency

 - Ownership: When we see a problem, we take ownership of it, and we solve it

 - Spirit: Clarity, optimism, meritocracy

What we offer:

 - Impact - Shape a company and redefine an industry. The team you build is the company.

 - Intensity - Complex problems, ownership, in-person in SF - we are here to solve problems and win.

 - Team - Everyone on our team considers themselves the most competitive person they have ever met - and we all come from the world’s best institutions.

 - Zero Barriers - We want the best people and will do whatever it takes. That includes full relocation support, covering all meals and paying for any product that makes you more productive.

 - Benefits - All meals covered; comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage; visa support.

source_jobs.title: Senior Events Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT STRALA

We deliver optimal claims outcomes to insurance companies. Not activity. Not output. Outcomes. Every product decision, operational improvement, and line of code is held to this standard.

We are rebuilding claims processing, with the ambition to process every claim in the world. That requires long-term thinking, world-class execution, and people committed to doing things the hard but correct way.

THE ROLE

We sell into large, established companies where claims are seen as a cost center and not a strategic business driver. Our sales cycles are long, our buyers are senior, they've been burned multiple times by empty promises, and trust is the currency that moves deals. Events are our most proven channel — and this role owns it.

We're looking for a Senior Events Manager who thinks in programs, not one-offs. You will own the full events motion from strategy through execution: deciding which events to invest in, building our owned event formats, and ensuring every touchpoint before, during, and after is moving the right people closer to a conversation with our team.

This is not a coordination role. You will be as comfortable developing a quarterly events strategy as you will be on-site managing a roundtable. You will be the first impression many of these buyers have of Strala. That comes with responsibility. You operate with ownership and will be given it.

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Build and manage our end-to-end events program — sponsored events, owned roundtables and dinners, and industry conferences

 - Develop the annual events strategy: which events, why, and what success looks like for each

 - Own pre-event outreach and targeting in close collaboration with sales to ensure the right buyers are in the room

 - Design the on-site and post-event experience to drive meaningful follow-up and pipeline progression

 - Own the full content and materials suite for events — briefs, leave-behinds, and follow-up sequences — in close alignment with marketing leadership

 - Manage event budgets, vendor relationships, and logistics end-to-end

 - Build repeatable playbooks so the events motion scales as we grow

 - Report on events performance against leading indicators tied to pipeline and relationships, not just attendance

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 6–8 years in event marketing, field marketing, or a closely related role — ideally in B2B with enterprise or financial services buyers

 - Demonstrated ownership of an events program, not just execution within one

 - Strong strategic instincts: you can articulate why an event belongs in the plan, not just how to run it

 - Exceptionally organized and calm under pressure — you manage complexity without losing the details

 - Strong communicator who can represent the company credibly in front of senior buyers

 - Experience with both third-party and owned event formats

 - Comfortable working in a lean team where you are both the strategist and the executor

NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in insurance, fintech, or selling into large enterprise companies

 - Familiarity with LinkedIn as a channel for pre- and post-event amplification

 - Experience building roundtable or hosted dinner programs from scratch

OUR VALUES

Ambition. We want to win and push for the highest standard.

Performance. We move fast, think in systems, and make decisions for the long term.

Ownership. When we see a problem, we step up and fix it.

Spirit. We operate with clarity, optimism, and meritocracy.

If this sounds like the role you've been waiting to own, we'd like to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT STRALA

We deliver optimal claims outcomes to insurance companies. Not activity. Not output. Outcomes. Every product decision, operational improvement, and line of code is held to this standard.

We are rebuilding claims processing — with the ambition to process every claim in the world. That requires long-term thinking, world-class execution, and people committed to doing things the hard but correct way.

THE ROLE

We sell into large, established companies where claims are seen as a cost center and not a strategic business driver. Our buyers are senior, our sales cycles are long, and trust is the currency that moves deals. Growth at Strala is not about volume; it is about precision.

We are building a marketing function from the ground up and we need someone who rigorously tracks performance so we can make confident decisions about what to do next. This role owns measurement and experimentation first, with the expectation of some channel execution.

This is a role for someone who is rigorously analytical first, and a marketer second. You will build the measurement system that the growth function runs on, and you will be honest about what the data does and does not tell us.

WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Build and maintain marketing measurement infrastructure — UTM tracking, CRM source tagging, pipeline attribution, and channel reporting

 - Design experiments across channels with clear hypotheses, controlled setups, and honest readouts regardless of outcome

 - Report on marketing performance with intellectual honesty — what is working, what is not, and what we cannot yet conclude

 - Analyze campaign and events data to surface insights that inform budget allocation and channel prioritization

 - Support channel execution, such as LinkedIn campaign set up, as the operational deployment layer

 - Define and standardize funnel metrics so marketing, sales, and leadership are working from the same definitions

 - Identify gaps in our measurement before we spend more money on channels we cannot yet evaluate

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 0–3 years of experience in an analytical role — the background matters less than the rigor you bring to it

 - You start with the data and let it shape your conclusions, not the other way around

 - Strong grasp of experimentation design — you know what a clean test looks like and why sample size and control conditions matter

 - Comfortable working with marketing analytics tools and CRM platforms

 - Intellectually honest — you are as comfortable reporting a null result as a positive one

 - Clear communicator — you can explain what the data shows and what it does not show to a non-technical audience

 - Comfort with ambiguity — you can make directional recommendations when the data is thin without overstating your confidence

NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in insurance, finance, or fintech

 - Experience with CRM platforms and marketing automation

 - Experience building measurement infrastructure from scratch rather than inheriting a working system

OUR VALUES

Ambition. We want to win and push for the highest standard.

Performance. We move fast, think in systems, and make decisions for the long term.

Ownership. When we see a problem, we step up and fix it.

Spirit. We operate with clarity, optimism, and meritocracy.

If you're looking for a role where you can build something real, we'd like to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: Strala is rebuilding the foundation of the world’s largest industry - insurance.

In less than a year, we reached multi seven-figure ARR and are growing 50% MOM. We raised two rounds from Tier 1 funds including Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, led by the partner who was early in OpenAI, Cognition and Scale AI. Our team comes from institutions like Harvard, Berkeley & Oxford and firms like Optiver, AMD & Palantir (+ICPC, NeurIPS backgrounds).




What we are looking for

 - Problem Solver - You love solving hard problems (and have a history of doing so).

 - High Agency & Ownership - You take charge and force things into existence - bonus points if you've been a CTO or founding engineer.

 - Proven Builder - 2+ years in backend development and Python, with a track record of fast, production-grade code shipping.

 - Core System Design - You own and are responsible for our core infrastructure, databases (PostgreSQL, vector DBs), and architecture.

 - Cloud Mastery - You know your way around REST, GraphQL, GCP, AWS, Docker.



Your First Month at Strala

 - Ship from Day 1 - You deploy to production on your first day and take full responsibility for your code.

 - Ownership - You create specs, you figure shit out, you build features.

 - Solve Every Problem - Hiring your next teammate, designing core infrastructure, scaling the existing codebase.



Values

 - Ambition: We want to win, and we need to be the best

 - Performance: Speed, system thinking, efficiency

 - Ownership: When we see a problem, we take ownership of it, and we solve it

 - Spirit: Clarity, optimism, meritocracy



We Offer

 - Impact - Shape a company, redefine an industry, and make your code impact millions.

 - Intensity - Complex problems, ownership, in-person in SF - we are here to solve problems and win.

 - Reward - Interesting work, top quartile salary, top quartile equity.

 - Team - Everyone on our team considers themselves the most competitive and intense person they have ever met.

 - Zero Barriers - We want the best people and will do whatever it takes. That includes covering all meals and paying for any product that makes you more productive.

source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: Strala is looking for the top 0.1% operators to do the hardest job in AI-native services: enabling mission-critical AI to work for the world’s largest insurers. This is a high-stakes, customer deployment role where you own the intersection of technical architecture and executive alignment. You will live inside carrier environments and will bridge the gap between our engineering and the messy, political, and technical reality of large carriers to deliver undeniable ROI. If you are a technical athlete who can lead a war room of demanding executives and then dive into a data model to solve a bottleneck, this is the role.



Background: Strala is automating claims and improving loss ratios for the world’s largest insurers. We are rebuilding the $280B foundation of the world's largest industry with applied AI.

In less than a year, we have reached multi-seven-figure ARR and ~50% MoM growth with zero dedicated growth headcount. In our first year we raised two rounds from Tier 1 funds led by the early partners in OpenAI, Cognition, and Ramp. Our lean team is a concentrated unit of top tier competitors: the top industry sales leaders, engineers from DRW, Optiver, AWS, and Palantir; AI researchers from Oxford and Cambridge labs publishing at ICML, ICCV, and NeurIPS; and multiple ICPC and Olympiad winners.



In this role you will

 - Own multiple client deployments from post-sale design through go-live and stabilization – and be the single point of contact for VP-level stakeholders throughout.

 - Work with client teams on architecture, data models, workflow design, and source-of-record decisions.

 - Turn messy client reality into clear plans: milestones, owners, risks, dependencies, and cadence. Communicate progress and blockers to client executives and internal engineering with equal precision.

 - Build trust with senior stakeholders while going deep with operators and technical teams. You are credible in both rooms.

 - Coordinate daily with Strala's engineering and sales teams – translating client needs into engineering priorities and deployment context into deal strategy.

 - Partner with Sales on late-stage deals to shape scope and create paths to expansion.

SF-based. We believe building together in person drives better outcomes.



You will thrive if you

 - Like being in the room with demanding clients and bringing order to ambiguity

 - Can move seamlessly between technical detail and executive communication

 - Think in systems, not tasks

 - Have strong judgment, high standards, and real ownership

 - Want to build a function, not just operate inside one

 - Care about making every deployment excellent because every deployment changes how claims get handled in the real world



Requirements

 - 2 to 5 years in roles combining technical depth, structured problem-solving, and client ownership

 - Exceptional communication and client presence

 - Strong structured thinking – you can break a complex implementation into a clear execution plan and drive it to completion

 - High agency and high slope

 - Likely backgrounds include: consulting / deployment / solutions / implementation in a high-performance environment; CS or similarly technical degree, software engineering internship or early engineering experience

 - Claims experience is irrelevant. You do need to be genuinely technical, highly structured, and strong with clients



Values

 - Ambition: We want to win, and we need to be the best

 - Performance: Speed, system thinking, efficiency

 - Ownership: When we see a problem, we take ownership of it, and we solve it

 - Spirit: Clarity, optimism, meritocracy



We Offer

 - Impact - Lead deployments at major insurers and Fortune 500s, improving a core workflow and directly growing ARR.

 - Build - Help write the playbook, standards, and operating rhythm that define how Strala scales.

 - Range - Combine technical depth, client ownership, and strategic problem-solving across every implementation.

 - AI at the fr

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: We are scaling from $XM to $XXM ARR and looking for winners to work directly with our CEO and SVP of Sales. This is an entirely untapped $280B market where our first two sellers just closed $3.8M in three months. We are moving $500K ACV contracts with a sub-100-day sales cycle - unmatched velocity for anyone hungry, aggressive, and competitive. If you perform, you’ll be fast-tracked for promotion.



Background: Strala is rebuilding the foundation of the world’s largest industry - insurance.
In less than a year, we reached multi seven-figure ARR and are growing 50% MoM. We raised two rounds from Tier 1 funds including Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, led by the partner who was early in OpenAI, Cognition and Scale AI. Our team comes from institutions like Harvard, Berkeley & Oxford and firms like Optiver, AMD & Palantir (+ICPC, NeurIPS backgrounds).



In this role you will

 - Drive pipeline for massive, AI-enabled enterprise service contracts ($500k+ ACV)

 - Join a team where two sellers closed > $3M in the first 3 months

 - Own outbound across cold calls, email automation, LinkedIn, conferences, and any channel that books meetings

 - Build and aggressively expand target account lists aligned to our ICP

 - Run first discovery calls and qualify high-value revenue opportunities

 - Work directly with senior sellers to convert conversations into real ARR

 - Build new playbooks, test new channels, and scale what works - as one of the first SDR hires, you will help build the outbound engine behind our enterprise growth



You will thrive if you

 - Are hungry, resilient, and see winning as the only acceptable option

 - Don’t get discouraged by rejection or high outbound volume

 - Communicate clearly and confidently (written + verbal)

 - Take ownership and build instead of waiting for instructions

 - Think in systems and constantly optimize for performance



Requirements

 - 1-2+ years in SDR, business development, GTM, or similarly performance-driven roles

 - Proven ability to generate outbound pipeline

 - Comfortable with cold calling and live conversations

 - High agency, strong organization, and willingness to travel for events



Values

 - Ambition: We want to win, and we need to be the best

 - Performance: Speed, system thinking, efficiency

 - Ownership: When we see a problem, we take ownership of it, and we solve it

 - Spirit: Clarity, optimism, meritocracy



We Offer

 - Impact - Direct impact on ARR, driving > $500k+ ACV enterprise deals.

 - Intensity - High standards, high velocity, in-person in SF.

 - Reward - Top quartile OTE, uncapped upside, equity.

 - Career Acceleration - Early sales hire with a fast path to AE or GTM leadership.

 - Team - Everyone on our team considers themselves the most competitive and intense person they have ever met.

 - Zero Barriers - We want the best people and will do whatever it takes. That includes covering all meals and paying for any product that makes you more productive.

source_jobs.title: VP, Sales & Strategic Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: We are scaling from $XM to $XXM ARR and looking for winners to work directly with our CEO and SVP of Sales. This is an entirely untapped $280B market where our first two sellers just closed $3.8M in three months. We are moving $500K ACV contracts with a sub-100-day sales cycle - unmatched velocity for anyone hungry, aggressive, and competitive. If you perform, you’ll be fast-tracked for promotion.



Background: Strala is rebuilding the foundation of the world’s largest industry - insurance.

In less than a year, we reached multi seven-figure ARR and are growing 50% MoM. We raised two rounds from Tier 1 funds including Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, led by the partner who was early in OpenAI, Cognition and Scale AI. Our team comes from institutions like Harvard, Berkeley & Oxford and firms like Optiver, AMD & Palantir (+ICPC, NeurIPS backgrounds).



Role Description: As a sales leader at Strala, you will work alongside the CEO and the SVPs to run the full commercial motion - prospecting, discovery, RFP, negotiation, close, and long-term growth. The product already wins on speed, accuracy, and data availability, and market demand is strong; your focus is to turn that demand into accelerating revenue and set the cadence for the next phase of scale. Work with us as we scale this from $XM to $XXM ARR over the next year. Compensation is above market standard: a competitive base, a rich variable plan tied directly to bookings and retention, and an equity grant designed to become truly meaningful as the business grows. You will collaborate daily with the entire team and have the autonomy to pursue, close, and expand the relationships that will define Strala’s growth.



What You Will Do

Sell

 - Own new-logo and retention targets for P&C TPA services

 - Own sourcing and maintain a qualified pipeline with accurate, data-driven forecasts

 - Codify a repeatable go-to-market playbook and recruit sales talent as volume grows

Access and Brand

 - Leverage an established network of carrier, MGA, captive, and broker executives to open senior conversations

 - Build channel alliances with brokers, captive managers, and insurtech platforms

 - Represent Strala at conferences, webinars, and virtual forums to expand visibility and deal flow

Solution and Deal Execution

 - Lead discovery sessions, ROI modelling, RFP responses, finalist presentations, and contract negotiations

 - Translate indemnity, LAE, and cycle-time savings into compelling business cases

 - Structure MSAs with performance-based fees, clear SLAs, and compliance safeguards

Upsell and Expand

 - Act as executive sponsor through onboarding and ongoing business reviews

 - Identify and close cross-sell opportunities across additional claim lines, analytics, and geographies

 - Track account health metrics and mobilise resources proactively to protect renewals



Who You Are

 - Eight or more years selling claims, TPA or similar insurance solutions solutions, regularly exceeding multi-million-dollar quotas

 - Exceptionally driven to win and looking for the right firm to create outsized earnings

 - Trusted relationships with Chief Claims Officers and risk executives, able to secure senior meetings quickly

 - Skilled negotiator of seven-figure deals and complex MSAs

 - Proven RFP strategist from sourcing to finalist pitch, comfortable defending pricing and SLAs

 - Experience in P&C claims operations across auto, property, warranty and other lines

 - Willing to travel (50%+)

 - Player-coach mindset, eager to build and lead a high-performing team as revenue scales



Why This Role

 - Direct link between your performance and compensation in a $280B market with legacy players scoring under 40 NPS

 - Ground-floor ownership of sales with influence across product and team; accelerate your career faster than anywhere else

 - Join a world-class team from Google, Palantir, Airbnb, and Progressive, backed by three multi-billion-dollar funds behind SpaceX, Anthropic, Uber,

source_jobs.title: Strategy & Operations
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: We’re looking for an exceptionally structured, execution-focused operator to help us scale claims operations. You will work directly with our CEO to bring clarity and efficiency to the systems and processes that drive how insurance claims are handled at scale. This role fast-tracks you into leadership of a core business area: with real ownership and a mandate to build and lead a team on your own.



Background: Strala is rebuilding the foundation of the world’s largest industry - insurance.

In less than a year, we reached multi seven-figure ARR and are growing 50% MOM. We raised two rounds from Tier 1 funds including Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, led by the partner who was early in OpenAI, Cognition and Scale AI. Our team comes from institutions like Harvard, Berkeley & Oxford and firms like Optiver, AMD & Palantir (+ICPC, NeurIPS backgrounds).



Role requirements: 2+ years in MBB consulting, investment banking, private equity, or top-tier startup in a strategic projects/operations role. Located in or willing to relocate to San Francisco.



You'll Thrive If:

 - You are exceptionally intelligent, hard working and ready to move fast

 - You are data-driven, structured and have attention to detail

 - You combine creative, solution-oriented thinking with detail-oriented execution

 - You get genuinely excited by tackling diverse, complex problems, always bringing structure and clarity

 - You are great with people - customers, teammates, partners -, build trust quickly and communicate effectively across different stakeholders

 - You enjoy building bridges between teams such as claims, engineering and sales

 - You are able to switch context quickly between different high-impact initiatives



What the role entails:

 - You will own special projects and strategic initiatives, working directly with the CEO

 - Build and formalize claims operations as we scale rapidly (process, hiring, workflows)

 - Design scalable, data-driven processes that optimize our product workflows

 - Lead cross-functional problem solving across product, engineering, and business teams



Our values:

 - Ambition: We want to win, and we need to be the best

 - Performance: Speed, system thinking, efficiency

 - Ownership: When we see a problem, we take ownership of it, and we solve it

 - Spirit: Clarity, optimism, meritocracy



What we offer:

 - Impact - Shape a company and redefine an industry

 - Intensity - Complex problems, ownership, in-person in SF - we are here to solve problems and win.

 - Flexibility - Ability to partially shape your role and grow it as the company scales.

 - Reward - Interesting work, top quartile salary, equity.

 - Team - Everyone on our team considers themselves the most competitive person they have ever met - and we all come from the world’s best institutions.

 - Zero Barriers - We provide relocation support and pay for any product that makes you more productive.

 - Benefits - All meals covered ; comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage; visa support

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: Strala is rebuilding the foundation of the world’s largest industry - insurance.

In less than a year, we reached multi seven-figure ARR and are growing 50% MOM. We raised two rounds from Tier 1 funds including Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, led by the partner who was early in OpenAI, Cognition and Scale AI. Our team comes from institutions like Harvard, Berkeley & Oxford and firms like Optiver, AMD & Palantir (+ICPC, NeurIPS backgrounds).



In this role you will

 - You will individually deploy multi-modal, agentic AI systems into production with major business impact. You own client deployments end-to-end, from scoping through go-live, acting as the primary point of contact for senior client stakeholders.

 - Build and manage client relationships: understand challenges, align priorities, and become the trusted partner executives rely on to scale their AI adoption.

 - Design, build, and evolve AI agents that deliver optimal claims outcomes, including prompt engineering, model integration, and blitz prototyping using AI-assisted coding tools.

 - Work with applied AI on a daily basis. You scope and develop new features that allow our customers to scale while consistently improving claims results.

 - Help integrate our product into client environments, working hands-on with their tech teams on APIs and systems.

 - Coordinate with our core platform team to ensure smooth delivery and improve the product with actionable insights.

 - Capture and share best practices in internal playbooks to scale Forward Deployed Engineering.



You will thrive if you

 - Think and operate like a founder. You take ownership of problems, move fast, and figure it out, whether that means jumping on a client call or writing a quick integration.

 - Have 2+ years in a role combining technical depth with client-facing ownership (e.g. agent engineering, forward deployed engineering, growth engineer, or founding engineer/founder).

 - Have a solid technical foundation: comfort with Python, APIs, cloud platforms, and modern AI/ML tooling. You are genuinely technical and can build.

 - Are familiar with LLMs, prompt engineering, and agentic AI concepts, and comfortable with AI-assisted development workflows to ship solutions fast.

 - Excel at explaining the connection between complex technical concepts and business outcomes to both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Want to own client relationships, project outcomes, and write code.



Values

 - Ambition: We want to win, and we need to be the best

 - Performance: Speed, system thinking, efficiency

 - Ownership: When we see a problem, we take ownership of it, and we solve it

 - Spirit: Clarity, optimism, meritocracy



We Offer

source_jobs.title: Subrogation Claims Adjuster
source_jobs.description:
TL;DR: Strala is an AI-native Third-Party Administrator (TPA) purpose-built for P&C claims. We combine agentic AI with expert human oversight to deliver faster, more accurate and fully transparent claims handling - from first notice of loss to resolution. Our platform eliminates repetitive manual work, improves consistency, and generates structured claims data that feeds directly into underwriting and operational strategy. We are a team of insurance experts and AI engineers.

Background: Our AI-powered automation is already helping leading global insurers operate more efficiently across multiple lines of business - reducing friction, accelerating workflows, and improving outcomes. In under a year, we’ve scaled to multi-seven-figure ARR with ~50% month-over-month growth. We’ve raised two rounds from top-tier investors, including early partners behind companies like OpenAI, Cognition, and Ramp - giving us the capital, backing, and momentum to build a category-defining platform in insurance.

Role Description: As a member of Strala’s Claims team, you’ll report to and work alongside the VP of Claims to deliver world-class service to our clients. You’ll work remotely in a fast-paced, high-growth environment to build customer relationships, investigate claims, and drive fair, timely resolutions.

Strala’s mission is to eliminate non-value-added, repetitive work so adjusters can focus on what matters most—applying their professional judgment to deliver the best possible claims experience. You’ll have the support of a collaborative team and ongoing coaching as we scale our capabilities.



Qualifications:

 - 3+ years of experience handling P&C claims (strong generalist with subrogation experience)

 - Active Adjuster License

 - Familiar with estimate review and repair management

 - Detail-oriented, organized, and self-motivated

 - Collaborative, curious, and comfortable with change

notes: Strala is rebuilding the foundation of the world’s largest industry - insurance. High growth rate and funding from Tier 1 funds.
